title: What is Photometric Augmentation?
concept: Photometric augmentation randomly changes an image's pixel VALUES — brightness, contrast, color, and noise — while leaving every pixel's POSITION exactly where it was, teaching a CNN to handle different lighting and cameras.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What do geometric augmentations like flipping, rotating, and cropping actually change about a training image?

options.text: Where pixels sit in the frame (position), not their color or brightness
The color and brightness of every pixel, without moving anything
The image's file format, like JPEG to PNG

exercises.explain: Right — geometric augmentation repositions content. This lesson covers the other half: changing pixel values instead.

cards.title: Same cat, two different lights
cards.body: You photograph your cat by a sunny window at noon, then again at dusk with a lamp on. Same cat, same pose, same spot on the couch — but the colors and brightness are totally different. Nothing about where the cat is in the frame changed at all.

cards.title: What is photometric augmentation?
cards.body: Photometric augmentation randomly changes an image's pixel values — things like brightness, contrast, color, and noise — during training. It never moves, flips, or crops anything; every pixel stays exactly where it was.

cards.title: Repainting vs. rearranging furniture
cards.body: Geometric augmentation is like rearranging the furniture in a room — same couch, same lamp, just in different spots. Photometric augmentation is like repainting the walls and changing the light bulbs — nothing moves, but everything looks different.

cards.title: One stop sign, ten looks
cards.body: Take a single training photo of a stop sign. Photometric augmentation can darken it to look like dusk, wash it out to look overexposed, or shift its red slightly toward orange — all while the sign's shape, edges, and position in the photo never move a pixel.

cards.title: It's not just "geometric, but for color"
cards.body: Photometric augmentation isn't a color version of geometric augmentation — the two fight different problems. Geometric augmentation teaches a CNN that an object is the same object no matter its pose or framing. Photometric augmentation teaches a CNN that an object is the same object no matter the lighting or camera that captured it.

cards.title: Geometric vs. photometric augmentation
cards.body: Two different kinds of random change, two different jobs:

left.text:
Changes pixel POSITIONS
Flip, rotate, crop, shift
Builds pose/framing invariance

right.text:
Changes pixel VALUES
Brightness, contrast, color, noise
Builds lighting/camera invariance

exercises.prompt: Which change is an example of photometric augmentation?

options.text: Making an image of a dog slightly brighter and warmer in color
Rotating the image of the dog 15 degrees
Cropping in tighter on the dog's face
Flipping the image of the dog horizontally

exercises.explain: Right — brightness and color changes edit pixel values, not pixel positions.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Photometric augmentation can move an object to a different part of the frame.
exercises.explain: False. Photometric augmentation only edits pixel values (color/brightness/contrast/noise); moving content is a job for geometric augmentation.

exercises.sentence: Photometric augmentation edits pixel ___, while geometric augmentation edits pixel positions.
exercises.explain: Photometric augmentation changes pixel values like brightness and color, leaving positions untouched.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is the clearest photometric augmentation applied to a training photo?

options.text: The same photo, rendered slightly darker with a subtle blue color cast
The same photo, cropped to a smaller square

exercises.explain: Correct — darkening and shifting color are value changes, not position changes.

exercises.prompt: A teammate says: 'We already flip, rotate, and crop our training images, so our model should handle any lighting condition at deployment too.' What's the best response?

options.text: Not necessarily — geometric augmentation only varies pixel positions; without photometric augmentation (brightness, contrast, color, noise) the model never sees varied lighting or camera conditions during training
They're right, since any augmentation makes a model equally robust to every kind of real-world variation
They're right, because flipping an image also changes its brightness
They're wrong, because geometric augmentation should be removed entirely once photometric augmentation is added

exercises.explain: Exactly! Geometric and photometric augmentation solve different problems — a pipeline needs both. Next: brightness and contrast jitter, one of the most common photometric tricks.
